summary: Israel’s defence establishment announced a cease‑fire framework tied to Hezbollah’s disarmament south of the Litani River and the continued presence of IDF forces to the yellow line. Ground operations north of the Litani resulted in the killing of a Hezbollah gunman, seizure of a weapons cache and airstrikes on cells in Tyre and Shaqra. Hezbollah mortars struck a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member and wounding two others.
key_points: Israel’s defence minister called for opposition apology and outlined a cease‑fire conditioned on Hezbollah’s removal south of the Litani and IDF stay to the yellow line (Source: @almogboker78/17306).
IDF alerts reported hostile aircraft intrusion near Arab al‑Aramshe and Kfar Yuval (Source: @idf_telegram/24130).

Israeli troops killed a Hezbollah operative north of the Litani, seized dozens of weapons including anti‑aircraft missiles, and struck Hezbollah cells in Tyre and Shaqra (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Hezbollah fired mortar shells that hit a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member and injuring two (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).
An Israeli armor officer, 21‑year‑old Eitan Shmuel Lemberg, was killed in combat in south Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200990).
IDF forces attacked a police point at Al‑Shabia intersection in Gaza, causing several injuries (Source: @yediotnews25/130533).

Extensive IDF strikes hit southern Lebanon targets including Nabatieh, villages Tolin, Khirbat Silis, and Tibbaneh north of the Bofar stronghold, plus an airstrike in the Sour area (Source: @yediotnews25/130538).

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s defence minister outlined a cease‑fire tied to Hezbollah’s disarmament south of the Litani and IDF’s continued presence to the yellow line. Ground operations north of the Litani killed a Hezbollah gunman, seized a weapons cache and included airstrikes on Tyre and Shaqra. Hezbollah mortars struck a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member.

points: Iranian deputy foreign minister stated that any agreement with the United States is not final unless Iranian interests are considered and demanded 50% control of frozen assets after a memorandum of understanding (Source: @naya_foriraq/77027).

Defence Minister Israel Katz said opposition must apologise and highlighted a cease‑fire dependent on Hezbollah’s removal south of the Litani, with IDF remaining to the yellow line and freedom to strike Beirut if needed (Source: @almogboker78/17306).
IDF spokesperson reported alerts for hostile aircraft intrusion over Arab al‑Aramshe and Kfar Yuval, with details under investigation (Source: @idf_telegram/24130).

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appointed Brigadier‑General Guy Markizano as his military secretary, promoting him to lieutenant‑general and replacing the head of Mossad (Source: @inon_yttach/8579).
IDF chief of staff appointed Brigadier‑General Tal Politis as Washington liaison and Brigadier‑General Barak Hiram as head of the Operations Division (Source: @inon_yttach/8584).
IDF forces attacked a police checkpoint at Al‑Shabia intersection in central Gaza, resulting in several injuries among Palestinians (Source: @yediotnews25/130533).

Israeli troops killed a Hezbollah operative north of the Litani River, seized dozens of weapons including anti‑aircraft missiles, and later air‑struck Hezbollah cells in Tyre and Shaqra near Bint Jbeil (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Hezbollah fired mortar shells that struck a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member and wounding two others (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).
An Israeli armor officer, 21‑year‑old Eitan Shmuel Lemberg, was killed in combat in south Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200990).

Extensive IDF strikes hit southern Lebanon locations including Nabatieh, villages Tolin, Khirbat Silis, and Tibbaneh north of the Bofar stronghold, plus an airstrike in the Sour area (Source: @yediotnews25/130538).

key_developments: Israel’s defence minister outlined a cease‑fire conditioned on Hezbollah’s removal south of the Litani and IDF staying to the yellow line (Source: @almogboker78/17306).
IDF alerts reported hostile aircraft intrusion near Arab al‑Aramshe and Kfar Yuval (Source: @idf_telegram/24130).
Israeli troops killed a Hezbollah operative north of the Litani, seized weapons cache, and struck cells in Tyre and Shaqra (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Hezbollah mortars hit a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member and injuring two (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).
Israeli armor officer Eitan Shmuel Lemberg was killed in combat in south Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200990).
IDF attacked a police point at Al‑Shabia intersection in Gaza, causing several injuries (Source: @yediotnews25/130533).
IDF conducted extensive strikes in southern Lebanon targeting Nabatieh, Tolin, Khirbat Silis, Tibbaneh and the Sour area (Source: @yediotnews25/130538).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to engage Israeli forces south of the Litani, employing mortars and maintaining weapons caches, indicating sustained low‑intensity conflict. Israeli ground and air operations demonstrate capability to neutralize individual operatives and degrade Hezbollah logistics. The presence of hostile aircraft incursions near the northern border adds a volatile aerial dimension, while UNIFIL assets remain vulnerable to indirect fire.
sections.regional_response: Israel’s political leadership is linking any cease‑fire to Hezbollah’s disarmament and maintaining a forward presence to the yellow line, reflecting a hardline stance. Iran’s diplomatic statements emphasize protecting its interests in any US‑related agreements, hinting at broader regional calculations. Lebanese media reports of widespread IDF strikes suggest heightened pressure on Hezbollah strongholds, potentially influencing Lebanese internal dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of hostile aircraft incursions should be intensified to assess potential external support for anti‑Israeli elements. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah weapons caches north of the Litani is critical for pre‑emptive targeting. Coordination with UNIFIL is essential to mitigate collateral risks to international personnel. Israeli appointment of senior officers to key liaison roles may signal upcoming strategic shifts in operational planning.
